question: How many actions are in a turn?
expected_quote: A player gets 4 actions to spend on her turn
expected_chunk_contains: A player gets 4 actions
expected_answer: Each player takes 4 actions on their turn. Unused actions cannot be saved for later turns.

question: How does a shuttle flight work?
expected_quote: If your pawn is in a city with a Research Station, move it to any other city with a Research Station
expected_chunk_contains: Shuttle Flight
expected_answer: If your pawn is in a city with a Research Station, you can spend an action to move it to any other city that also has a Research Station.

question: How do I Discover A Cure?
expected_quote: discard 5 cards of the same color to cure the corresponding disease
expected_chunk_contains: discard 5 cards of the same color
expected_answer: Be in a city with a Research Station and discard 5 cards of the same color to cure the corresponding disease. The Scientist only needs 4 cards.

question: How does it work if an outbreak chain reaction causes a cube to go back into the city the outbreak started in?
expected_quote: each city may only outbreak once in each chain reaction
no city can outbreak more than once in each chain reaction
expected_chunk_contains: outbreak once in each chain reaction
expected_answer: A city can only outbreak once per chain reaction. The rulebook example shows that no cube is even added back to the originating city.

question: Do I shuffle all the cards of the infection deck together during an epidemic?
expected_quote: Don't shuffle these cards into the Infection Draw Pile
expected_chunk_contains: Don't shuffle these cards
expected_answer: No. During an epidemic you shuffle only the Infection Discard Pile and place it on top of the remaining Infection Draw Pile; the two piles are not shuffled together.

question: Do any roles get better special abilities throughout the game?
expected_quote: if the Medic at any time finds herself in a city that contains cubes of a disease that has been cured, she may immediately remove all of those cubes
expected_chunk_contains: Medic at any time finds herself
expected_answer: Yes. The Medic's ability strengthens once a disease is cured: she can immediately remove all cubes of a cured disease from any city she enters, on any player's turn, with no action cost.

question: Does the game end when you have to place the last cube of a color on the board?
expected_quote: A player needs to add disease cubes to the board and there aren't any left of that color in the supply
expected_chunk_contains: aren't any left of that color in the supply
expected_answer: No. The game only ends when you need to add a cube but the supply for that color is empty. Placing the last available cube is fine; failing to add one because none remain is the loss condition.

question: What is the hand limit and what happens if I exceed it?
expected_quote: Players have a hand limit of 7 cards
expected_chunk_contains: hand limit of 7 cards
expected_answer: The hand limit is 7 cards. If drawing or Share Knowledge brings you above 7, you must immediately discard the excess to the Player Discard Pile.

question: What happens if the Player Draw Pile runs out before we discover all the cures?
expected_quote: If there aren't enough cards in the Player Draw Pile to draw, the game immediately ends in defeat for all players
expected_chunk_contains: enough cards in the Player Draw Pile
expected_answer: The game immediately ends in defeat for all players if there aren't enough cards in the Player Draw Pile when you must draw.

question: What happens if I need to add cubes of a color but the supply for that color is empty?
expected_quote: A player needs to add disease cubes to the board and there aren't any left of that color in the supply
expected_chunk_contains: aren't any left of that color in the supply
expected_answer: The game immediately ends in defeat for all players.

question: How many cubes of one color can a city hold before adding another causes an outbreak?
expected_quote: An outbreak occurs if a player is required to add a cube to a city that already has 3 cubes in it of that color
expected_chunk_contains: city that already has 3 cubes
expected_answer: A city can hold up to 3 cubes of a single color. Adding a 4th cube of the same color triggers an outbreak instead.

question: What can the Dispatcher do that other players cannot?
expected_quote: He may also spend an action to move a pawn to any city that contains another pawn
expected_chunk_contains: spend an action to move a pawn to any city that contains another pawn
expected_answer: The Dispatcher can move other players' pawns using basic actions, and can spend an action to move any pawn directly to a city that already contains another pawn.

question: Does the Operations Expert need to play a city card to build a Research Station?
expected_quote: The Operations Expert does not have to play the card matching the city his pawn occupies
expected_chunk_contains: Operations Expert does not have to play the card
expected_answer: No. The Operations Expert is the exception: he simply spends an action to add a Research Station to his current city, without needing a card.

question: What happens when an infection card of an eradicated color is drawn?
expected_quote: cards of this color have no effect when drawn on the Infector's turn
If, however, the pictured city is of a color that has been eradicated, do not add a cube
expected_chunk_contains: no effect when drawn on the Infector's turn
of a color that has been eradicated, do not add a cube
expected_answer: Nothing happens. Once a disease is eradicated, infection cards of that color have no effect when drawn during the Infector phase.

question: When can Special Event cards be played?
expected_quote: These cards may be played at any time (even on a fellow player's turn) and do not require an action to play
expected_chunk_contains: may be played at any time
expected_answer: Special Event cards can be played at any time, even on another player's turn, and do not cost an action to play.

question: How many outbreaks does it take to lose the game?
expected_quote: If the number of outbreaks ever reaches 8 (and the Outbreaks Marker reaches the skull symbol), the game immediately ends in defeat for all players
expected_chunk_contains: number of outbreaks ever reaches 8
expected_answer: The game ends in defeat once the number of outbreaks reaches 8 (the skull symbol on the Outbreak Indicator).

question: When an Epidemic card is drawn, which infection card gets infected?
expected_quote: Take the bottom card from the Infection Draw Pile and add 3 cubes to the city pictured on the card
expected_chunk_contains: bottom card from the Infection Draw Pile
expected_answer: The bottom card of the Infection Draw Pile. You add 3 cubes to that city, then place the card in the Infection Discard Pile.

question: I'm the Medic and a cure has been discovered for Yellow but Yellow is not eradicated. I move into a city with 2 Yellow cubes during another player's turn. What happens?
expected_quote: if the Medic at any time finds herself in a city that contains cubes of a disease that has been cured, she may immediately remove all of those cubes
expected_chunk_contains: Medic at any time finds herself
expected_answer: Both Yellow cubes are immediately removed. Once Yellow is cured, the Medic clears every cube of that color from any city she enters, on any player's turn, with no action cost.

question: I'm in Tokyo and want to Charter Flight to Paris. I have the Paris card in my hand but not the Tokyo card. Can I do it?
expected_quote: Play the card corresponding to your pawn's current location, and move to any city on the board
expected_chunk_contains: Play the card corresponding to your pawn's current location
expected_answer: No. Charter Flight requires the card matching your current city (Tokyo here), not the destination. With only the Paris card you cannot Charter Flight from Tokyo.

question: I'm the Researcher in Moscow with another player. I have the London card. Can I give them my London card via Share Knowledge?
expected_quote: may give a fellow player any card from his hand when involved in a Share Knowledge action
expected_chunk_contains: Researcher may give a fellow player any card
expected_answer: Yes. The Researcher's special ability lets him give any card from his hand when giving via Share Knowledge — he is not restricted to the card of the shared city.

question: I have 7 cards in hand and my teammate gives me a card via Share Knowledge. What do I do?
expected_quote: the excess cards must be immediately discarded to the Player Discard Pile
the player must immediately discard cards in excess to the Player Discard Pile
expected_chunk_contains: excess cards must be immediately discarded
expected_answer: Immediately discard a card down to the 7-card hand limit. You choose which card to discard.

question: What do I do when I draw an Epidemic card during my draw step?
expected_quote: Whenever a player draws an Epidemic card, discard the card into the Player Discard Pile and do the following
expected_chunk_contains: Whenever a player draws an Epidemic card
expected_answer: Discard the Epidemic card to the Player Discard Pile and immediately resolve the Epidemic procedure: increase the infection rate, infect the bottom card of the Infection Draw Pile by adding 3 cubes, then shuffle the Infection Discard Pile and place it on top of the remaining Infection Draw Pile.

question: Can a single city outbreak more than once during one chain reaction?
expected_quote: each city may only outbreak once in each chain reaction
expected_chunk_contains: outbreak once in each chain reaction
expected_answer: No. Each city can only outbreak once per chain reaction.

question: How many rounds are in the game?
expected_quote: Players are participating in a 7-round tournament
expected_chunk_contains: 7-round tournament
expected_answer: There are 7 rounds (matches), followed by a final between the two players with the most fans. So a finalist plays 8 matches total.

question: When can I remove cards from my deck?
expected_quote: At the end of the Deck Phase you may remove any number of cards from your deck
expected_chunk_contains: end of the Deck Phase you may remove
expected_answer: At the end of the Deck Phase you may remove any number of cards from your deck.

question: Does a card still trigger Flag loss if it was on the bottom of the pile?
expected_quote: this card must have been in flag possession beforehand
expected_chunk_contains: must have been in flag possession beforehand
expected_answer: No. A Flag loss effect only triggers on the card that actually had the flag (the latest revealed card). Cards slid underneath never had the flag, so their Flag loss effects don't fire.

question: Who starts with the flag if both players won their last match?
expected_quote: If there is a tie, toss the flag
expected_chunk_contains: If there is a tie, toss the flag
expected_answer: If both players hold trophies and tie on highest round number, the tie is broken by a flag toss to decide who begins.

question: Which trophy do I get on round 1?
expected_quote: stacking them in descending order (7 at the bottom, and 1 on top)
expected_chunk_contains: descending order (7 at the bottom
expected_answer: The trophy with round number 1. Trophies are stacked in descending order with 7 at the bottom and 1 on top, so round 1 takes the top trophy.

question: What happens if my opponent has to bench cards but doesn't have enough empty seats on their bench?
expected_quote: Your opponent must put one or more cards on their bench but does not have enough empty seats available
expected_chunk_contains: does not have enough empty seats available
expected_answer: You win the match. Running out of empty bench seats is one of the two ways to win.

question: If a card gets an immediate power bonus during my attack, does the bonus persist when the card ends up in flag possession?
expected_quote: If a card gets an immediate power bonus, that card keeps it even in flag possession
expected_chunk_contains: card keeps it even in flag possession
expected_answer: Yes. A card that received an immediate power bonus keeps that bonus when it ends up in flag possession.

question: Can I look through my deck or rearrange its order during a match?
expected_quote: you must not look at your cards or rearrange their order
expected_chunk_contains: must not look at your cards or rearrange their order
expected_answer: No. During the Match Phase you must not look at your cards or rearrange their order, unless an effect explicitly allows it.

question: Are the fan values on my trophies visible to the other players?
expected_quote: Keep it hidden from other players
expected_chunk_contains: Keep it hidden from other players
expected_answer: No. The back of each trophy shows its fan value; you keep it hidden from other players.

question: Is there a way to win immediately in a 2-player game without finishing all 7 rounds?
expected_quote: You win the game immediately if you have at least 11 fans more than your opponent at the end of a Match Phase
expected_chunk_contains: at least 11 fans more than your opponent
expected_answer: Yes. In a 2-player game, you win immediately if you have at least 11 more fans than your opponent at the end of any Match Phase.

question: Do the two finalists draw and pick new cards before the final match begins?
expected_quote: Before the final starts, you do not draw and pick new cards
expected_chunk_contains: do not draw and pick new cards
expected_answer: No. Finalists do not draw and pick new cards before the final, although they may still remove cards from their deck as in the Deck Phase.

question: I drew 5 cards in the Deck Phase and didn't like any of them. What can I do?
expected_quote: Once per Deck Phase you may discard the cards drawn this round and draw that number of new cards from the same Level Pile
expected_chunk_contains: Once per Deck Phase you may discard
expected_answer: Once per Deck Phase you may discard all of the cards you drew this round and draw the same number of new cards from the same Level Pile.

question: When does an effect labelled "From the bench" apply?
expected_quote: Their effect applies as soon as this card is on the bench and is active as long as it remains on the bench
expected_chunk_contains: as soon as this card is on the bench
expected_answer: A 'From the bench' effect applies as soon as the card is on the bench and stays active as long as the card remains on the bench.

question: If a card has a Flag loss effect and the card that's about to take the flag has an In flag possession effect, which effect resolves first?
expected_quote: Flag loss effects are resolved prior to effects that apply when a card gets in flag possession
expected_chunk_contains: Flag loss effects are resolved prior to
expected_answer: The Flag loss effect resolves first. Flag loss effects are resolved prior to effects that apply when a card gets in flag possession.

question: I'm attacking. My revealed cards total exactly 5 power. My opponent's flag card has 5 power. Do I take the flag?
expected_quote: As soon as the total power of your card(s) is equal to or higher than the total power of the card of your opponent, take the flag
expected_chunk_contains: equal to or higher than the total power
expected_answer: Yes. Equal total power is enough — you take the flag the moment your total is equal to or greater than the defender's.

question: My second revealed card already gives me enough total power to take the flag. Can I keep revealing more cards anyway to put a stronger card in flag possession?
expected_quote: You must not reveal more cards than needed to get in flag possession
expected_chunk_contains: must not reveal more cards than needed
expected_answer: No. You must not reveal more cards than needed to get in flag possession; the latest revealed card is the one that ends up in flag possession.

question: Three players are tied for second place in fans after round 7. How is the tie broken to decide who plays in the final?
expected_quote: Break ties in favor of the tied player who won more Trophies
expected_chunk_contains: tied player who won more Trophies
expected_answer: First, in favor of whichever tied player won more trophies. If still tied, in favor of whoever holds the single trophy with the highest round number.

question: The first card revealed at the start of a match has an immediate effect printed on it. Do I resolve the effect, or does it only matter for cards revealed during attack?
expected_quote: If the first revealed card that begins a match has an effect, do not forget to resolve it
expected_chunk_contains: first revealed card that begins a match
expected_answer: Yes, you resolve the effect. The rulebook explicitly says not to forget the effect on the first revealed card that begins a match.

question: How many white chips until you bust?
expected_quote: If the sum of all white chips drawn exceeds the value of 7, you have too many cherry bombs in your potion and the pot explodes
expected_chunk_contains: sum of all white chips drawn exceeds the value of 7
expected_answer: Your pot explodes when the sum of values on the white chips you've drawn exceeds 7.

question: How many turns are in a game?
expected_quote: The game consists of 9 turns
expected_chunk_contains: game consists of 9 turns
expected_answer: The game lasts 9 turns.

question: What chips do I start the game with in my bag?
expected_quote: 4 white 1-chips, 2 white 2-chips, 1 white 3-chip, 1 orange 1-chip and 1 green 1-chip
expected_chunk_contains: 4 white 1-chips
expected_answer: Each player starts with 4 white 1-chips, 2 white 2-chips, 1 white 3-chip, 1 orange 1-chip, and 1 green 1-chip in their bag.

question: When does the yellow ingredient book come into play?
expected_quote: Before beginning turn 2, lay out the yellow ingredient book
expected_chunk_contains: Before beginning turn 2, lay out the yellow
expected_answer: The yellow ingredient book is laid out before turn 2 begins.

question: When does the purple ingredient book come into play?
expected_quote: Before turn 3, lay out the purple ingredient book
expected_chunk_contains: Before turn 3, lay out the purple
expected_answer: The purple ingredient book is laid out before turn 3.

question: Do I need to add anything to my bag before turn 6?
expected_quote: Before beginning turn 6, each player has to put 1 more white 1-chip into their bag
expected_chunk_contains: put 1 more white 1-chip into their bag
expected_answer: Yes. Before turn 6 begins, each player adds 1 more white 1-chip to their bag.

question: When can players start using rats, and is anyone excluded?
expected_quote: Everyone, except for the player in the lead, can throw rat-tails into their pots
expected_chunk_contains: Everyone, except for the player in the lead
expected_answer: Rats come into play starting in round 2. Every player except the one currently in the lead can use them.

question: Can I use my flask if the chip I just drew caused my pot to explode?
expected_quote: If the last chip drawn causes the pot to explode, the flask cannot be used
expected_chunk_contains: If the last chip drawn causes the pot to explode, the flask cannot be used
expected_answer: No. If the chip you just drew caused your pot to explode, the flask cannot be used.

question: How many chips can I buy each turn and what are the restrictions?
expected_quote: You may buy 1 or 2 chips. If buying 2 chips, they cannot be the same color
expected_chunk_contains: buy 1 or 2 chips
expected_answer: You may buy 1 or 2 chips per turn. If buying 2, they cannot be the same color.

question: What happens if my chip lands on or past space 33?
expected_quote: You receive 15 victory points and can go shopping with 35 coins
expected_chunk_contains: 15 victory points and can go shopping with 35 coins
expected_answer: Place the chip on the 33 space, then take the spoon bonus: 15 victory points and 35 coins to shop with. If your pot didn't explode, you also roll the Bonus Die.

question: If my pot exploded, can I still both score victory points and buy chips this turn?
expected_quote: Players whose pot exploded now have to chose between either Evaluation Phase D or E
expected_chunk_contains: chose between either Evaluation Phase D or E
expected_answer: No. If your pot exploded, you must choose between Phase D (victory points) or Phase E (buying chips), not both.

question: How is the winner decided if multiple players are tied for first place at game end?
expected_quote: the one who was able to fill up their pot the most in the last turn wins
expected_chunk_contains: fill up their pot the most in the last turn
expected_answer: Whoever filled their pot the most in the last turn wins. If still tied, all tied players share the title of biggest quack.

question: Can I buy victory points at the end of the game?
expected_quote: you can decide to buy a victory point with either 5 coins or 2 rubies
expected_chunk_contains: buy a victory point with either 5 coins or 2 rubies
expected_answer: Yes. At the end of the last turn, you may buy victory points at a cost of 5 coins or 2 rubies each, and you can repeat this as many times as you like.

question: Am I allowed to look inside my bag while drawing chips?
expected_quote: You are never allowed to look into your bag
expected_chunk_contains: never allowed to look into your bag
expected_answer: No. You are never allowed to look into your bag.

question: Does a player whose pot exploded get to roll the bonus die?
expected_quote: Any player whose pot has exploded does not get to roll the die in Evaluation Phase A
expected_chunk_contains: pot has exploded does not get to roll the die
expected_answer: No. A player whose pot exploded does not roll the Bonus Die in Evaluation Phase A.

question: How much does it cost to refill my flask?
expected_quote: Players can fill up their flasks at the cost of 2 rubies
expected_chunk_contains: fill up their flasks at the cost of 2 rubies
expected_answer: It costs 2 rubies to refill your flask, done at the end of a turn.

question: My white chips drawn so far total exactly 7. Has my pot exploded?
expected_quote: If the sum of all white chips drawn exceeds the value of 7, you have too many cherry bombs in your potion and the pot explodes
expected_chunk_contains: sum of all white chips drawn exceeds the value of 7
expected_answer: No. The pot only explodes when the white-chip sum exceeds 7, so exactly 7 is safe. You can stop or risk drawing more.

question: Two players reached the exact same highest scoring space without exploding. Who rolls the Bonus Die?
expected_quote: If there is a tie, they all get to roll the die
expected_chunk_contains: If there is a tie, they all get to roll the die
expected_answer: Both of them. If multiple non-exploded players are tied for the highest scoring space, they all roll the Bonus Die.

question: I'm in second place with no rat-tails between me and the leader on the Scoring Track. What does that mean for my rat stone this turn?
expected_quote: each player counts the number of rat-tails between them and the leading player on the Scoring Track
the blue player cannot use their rat stone in this round
expected_chunk_contains: counts the number of rat-tails between them and the leading player
expected_answer: Your rat stone effectively can't be used this turn — it would advance zero spaces. The rat stone moves forward by the number of rat-tails between you and the leader, and there are none.

question: Mid-Potions-Phase, can I spend 2 rubies to move my droplet forward right now to put my next chip on a higher space?
expected_quote: At the end of the turn, players can carry out the following actions (more than once) by spending rubies
trade in 2 rubies and move their droplet 1 space forward
expected_chunk_contains: At the end of the turn, players can carry out the following actions
expected_answer: No. Spending rubies to move your droplet forward only happens at the end of the turn (Phase F), not during the Potions Phase. The benefit applies to subsequent turns.

question: Can I buy two chips of the same color this turn if I want to stock up?
expected_quote: If buying 2 chips, they cannot be the same color
expected_chunk_contains: If buying 2 chips, they cannot be the same color
expected_answer: No. If you buy 2 chips, they cannot be the same color.
